concepts.title: The mirror pair statement
concepts.blurb: For a Calabi–Yau 3-fold $X$ there is conjecturally a mirror partner $Y$ — another CY 3-fold — with the Hodge numbers swapped: $h^{1,1}(X) = h^{2,1}(Y)$ and $h^{2,1}(X) = h^{1,1}(Y)$. Geometrically this exchanges the Kähler (symplectic) moduli of $X$ with the complex-structure moduli of $Y$. Physically it identifies the type-IIA string on $X$ with the type-IIB string on $Y$, sending the A-model on $X$ to the B-model on $Y$.

concepts.title: The quintic prediction
concepts.blurb: Candelas–de la Ossa–Green–Parkes (1991) computed periods on the mirror $Y$ of the quintic 3-fold $X \subset \mathbb{P}^4$ and read off, by mirror symmetry, the Gromov–Witten invariants of $X$ — predicting the count of degree-$d$ rational curves on the quintic for every $d$. The numbers (e.g. $2{,}875$ lines, $609{,}250$ conics, $317{,}206{,}375$ twisted cubics, $\dots$) were later proven to all orders by Givental and Lian–Liu–Yau, the first hard mathematical confirmation of the conjecture.

concepts.title: A-model: Gromov–Witten invariants
concepts.blurb: The A-model on $(X,\omega)$ is governed by counts of pseudo-holomorphic curves: stable maps $f\colon \Sigma_g \to X$ from a genus-$g$ Riemann surface representing a class $\beta \in H_2(X,\mathbb{Z})$. The genus-zero Gromov–Witten invariants $N_\beta = \int_{[\overline{\mathcal{M}}_{0,0}(X,\beta)]^{\mathrm{vir}}} 1$ assemble into a generating function depending only on the Kähler class $[\omega]$ — symplectic data, not complex-structure data.

concepts.title: B-model: periods and Picard–Fuchs
concepts.blurb: The B-model on $Y$ is governed by the holomorphic 3-form $\Omega \in H^{3,0}(Y)$. Its periods $\Pi_\gamma(t) = \int_\gamma \Omega(t)$ over a basis $\gamma \in H_3(Y,\mathbb{Z})$ satisfy a Picard–Fuchs ODE in the complex-structure parameter $t$. Solving Picard–Fuchs gives the periods as hypergeometric series; the mirror map $t \mapsto q$ is the change of coordinates that turns these series into A-model Gromov–Witten generating functions.

concepts.title: Homological mirror symmetry (Kontsevich)
concepts.blurb: Kontsevich's 1994 ICM proposal: mirror symmetry is an equivalence of triangulated categories $D^b\mathrm{Coh}(X) \cong D^\pi\mathrm{Fuk}(Y)$. The B-model on $X$ is the bounded derived category of coherent sheaves; the A-model on $Y$ is the (split-closed) Fukaya category, whose objects are Lagrangian submanifolds equipped with brane data and whose hom-spaces are Lagrangian intersection Floer cohomologies. Verified for elliptic curves, quartic and quintic 3-folds, toric varieties, and many more.

concepts.title: Strominger–Yau–Zaslow fibrations
concepts.blurb: The Strominger–Yau–Zaslow conjecture (1996) gives a geometric mechanism for mirror symmetry: a CY 3-fold $X$ admits a special-Lagrangian $T^3$-fibration $X \to B$ over a real 3-dimensional base, and the mirror $Y \to B$ is the dual fibration — fibrewise replace each torus $T^3_b$ by its dual $\check T^3_b = \mathrm{Hom}(T^3_b, U(1))$. T-duality on the fibres swaps A-model and B-model. A foundation for both HMS and the modern Gross–Siebert program.
